args.query: "codecov yml validation schema spec comment layout behavior require_changes"

content:
Created At: 2026-06-09T16:00:15Z
Completed At: 2026-06-09T16:00:19Z
The search for "codecov yml validation schema spec comment layout behavior require_changes" returned the following summary:
In Codecov, the `comment` section in your `codecov.yml` file allows you to customize the pull request comment. You can control which information is displayed and the conditions under which the comment is posted.

### Configuration Overview
To configure the PR comment, use the top-level `comment` key.

```yaml
comment:
  layout: "header, diff, flags, files"
  behavior: default
  require_changes: false
```

### Key Settings
*   **`layout`**: Defines the components shown in the PR comment. You can choose the order and types of components to include. Commonly available components include:
    *   `header`, `diff`, `flags`, `files`, `reach`, `tree`, `components`
    *   You can also add `condensed_` prefixes (e.g., `condensed_header`) to certain components to change their visual density.
    *   *Note:* If you omit a component, it will not appear in the comment.
*   **`behavior`**: Determines the posting logic (e.g., `default`, which typically creates a new comment or updates an existing one).
*   **`require_changes`**: A boolean (or specific logic string) that controls when the comment is posted.
    *   If set to `false` (default), the comment will be posted regardless of coverage changes.
    *   If set to `true`, the comment will only be posted if there is a change in coverage.
*   **Additional Options**:
    *   `require_base`: (Boolean) If `true`, requires a base report to be present to post the comment.
    *   `require_head`: (Boolean) If `true`, requires a head report to be present to post the comment.
    *   `hide_project_coverage`: (Boolean) If `true`, hides project-wide coverage and only displays diff/patch coverage.

### Validation
Because YAML files are sensitive to indentation and syntax, it is highly recommended to validate your `codecov.yml` file before deploying changes. You can do this by sending your file to the Codecov validation API:

```bash
curl -X POST --data-binary @codecov.yml https://api.codecov.io/validate
```

You can also use the **Codecov VS Code extension** for real-time validation and autocomplete while editing your configuration.
I will validate the `codecov.yml` file using Codecov's official validation API to verify its syntax.
